title: Tax Loss Harvesting - Short-Term Capital Losses

requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must strategically sell securities held 12 months or less at a loss to offset short-term capital gains taxed at ordinary income rates, providing greater tax benefit per dollar of loss than long-term loss harvesting
criteria: Identify positions held 12 months or less trading below cost basis with unrealized short-term capital losses
Sell losing positions before 12-month anniversary to realize short-term character if beneficial
Short-term capital losses first offset short-term capital gains (taxed at ordinary rates up to 37%)
After offsetting STCG, STCL offsets long-term capital gains, then up to $3,000 ordinary income
Excess losses carry forward indefinitely maintaining character as short-term losses
Avoid wash sale rule by not repurchasing substantially identical security within 61-day window
Higher tax benefit than LTCL when offsetting STCG taxed at ordinary rates vs preferential LTCG rates
Coordinate with holding periods to maximize tax benefit of loss character vs gain character

stcl_harvesting_strategy.loss_ordering: STCL offsets STCG first (ordinary rates), then LTCG (preferential rates), then $3,000 ordinary
stcl_harvesting_strategy.higher_tax_benefit: STCL saves up to 40.8% (37% + 3.8% NIIT) when offsetting STCG vs 23.8% for LTCG
stcl_harvesting_strategy.carryforward_character: Unused STCL carries forward indefinitely as short-term maintaining higher benefit potential
stcl_harvesting_strategy.timing_flexibility: Can harvest STCL anytime; selling before 12 months preserves short-term character
stcl_harvesting_strategy.portfolio_rebalancing: Use loss harvesting during rebalancing to reduce tax drag on portfolio returns
stcl_harvesting_strategy.strategic_character: Consider whether to harvest as ST or wait for LT based on expected future gains

restrictions: Wash sale rule applies same as long-term losses - no repurchase within 61-day window
Substantially identical security analysis same for short-term and long-term positions
Holding period calculation exact: 12 months or less = short-term; over 12 months = long-term
Trade date controls holding period, not settlement date
STCL may be better used to offset STCG; consider timing of harvesting relative to expected gains
Loss on sale to related party disallowed under §267 regardless of holding period
IRS may challenge if pattern of harvesting losses while gains escape recognition (tax-motivated trading)
State tax conformity varies; some states don't distinguish ST vs LT requiring state analysis
